title: TraceProjection API (HBR-VIS-004 non-UI visual verification)
goal: Per spec §6.5 + HBR-VIS-004, implement the TraceProjection read API: a single GET endpoint `/inspector/v1/trace/{session_id}` returning a structured reconstruction of `what task / what context / what return / what tool calls / what artifact / what validation / what promotion` for a session, derived purely from durable state (no chat history, no terminal scrollback, no provider memory).
expected_diff_shape: Implementation of TraceProjection in src/backend/handshake_core/src/inspector_read/trace_projection.rs; wired into MT-028 trait + MT-029 server; tests verify reconstruction from a synthetic session's EventLedger.
unblock_conditions: MT-028 trait + MT-029 server scaffold ship; EventLedger primitive available from KERNEL-001.
implementation_notes: Per HBR-VIS-004 evidence_kind=trace_projection_inspection. TraceProjection schema: `{ session_id, opened_at, closed_at: Option, what_task: { wp_id, mt_id, task_summary }, what_context: { context_bundle_id, context_summary, hash }, what_returns: Vec<{ event_id, content_summary, hash }>, what_tool_calls: Vec<{ tool_name, args_hash, result_summary, gate_pass: bool }>, what_artifacts: Vec<{ artifact_id, kind, path, sha256 }>, what_validation: Vec<{ check_id, verdict, evidence_pointer }>, what_promotion: Option<{ promotion_event_id, gate_id, verdict }> }`. The projection is reconstructed from EventLedger rows for the session — no provider memory, no chat scrollback. Implementation: scan EventLedger for rows with parent_session_id == session_id, classify each event by kind (TASK_OPEN, CONTEXT_BUNDLE_DISPATCH, MODEL_RESPONSE, TOOL_CALL_REQUEST, ARTIFACT_WRITTEN, VALIDATION_RESULT, PROMOTION_EVENT), fold into the structured projection. Hashes: content_summary carries first 200 chars; hash field is sha256 of full content (so summary can be trusted to identify uniquely). Tests: spawn a synthetic session via KERNEL-001 EventLedger fixtures, fire one of each event kind, query the projection, assert all seven fields are populated correctly. Negative: session_id with no events returns 404 (not empty projection — that would be misleading).
risk_if_missed: Without TraceProjection, HBR-VIS-004 has no surface; non-UI work like sandbox/runtime/memory cannot be visually verified.
validator_focus: Projection is reconstructed purely from EventLedger (no chat-history or scrollback leakage); 404 on empty rather than empty-projection; content_summary hashes are sha256 of full content not truncated.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Reconstruction sourced exclusively from EventLedger (no chat-history input).
Empty session returns 404 (not a misleading empty projection).
Hashes are full-content sha256 (not summary hash, which would be ambiguous).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: TraceProjection reconstructs all seven HBR-VIS-004 fields purely from EventLedger rows (or KernelEvent), returns None for empty sessions (mapped to 404 by the server), and hashes full content rather than truncated summaries — the spec invariant 'reconstructable from durable state alone' holds.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: TraceProjection reconstructs all seven HBR-VIS-004 fields purely from EventLedger rows (or KernelEvent), returns None for empty sessions (mapped to 404 by the server), and hashes full content rather than truncated summaries — the spec invariant 'reconstructable from durable state alone' holds.
